---
title: "Open-science initiatives and large-scale collaborations"
root: ".."
---

::: {.callout-note}
This page is a **scaffold**. Replace placeholder text with your own synthesis and citations.
:::

## Open datasets and open science

*Placeholder:* Discuss:
- Data sharing as a mechanism to improve representativeness and reproducibility
- Risks: privacy, re-identification, extractive practices
- Governance patterns: DACs, DUAs, federated models

## Shared atlases and multi-site harmonisation

*Placeholder:* Summarise:
- Why harmonisation matters for multi-site diversity
- How harmonisation can both reduce and hide bias (important nuance)
- Common evaluation patterns for harmonisation (e.g., preserve biological signal)

## Large-scale collaborations

*Placeholder:* Provide an overview of major collaborations and what they enable:
- Sample size & power
- Cross-site generalisation
- Multimodal integration

## What to link here

This hub site is meant to link to a dedicated, reproducible repository:

- **Shared atlases & harmonisation pipelines** (external repo)
- **Dataset inventory** (external repo)

You can update the “Original research” section when those repos are created.
